name: Rachaad White
headline: Fading committee back changes teams, flex ceiling capped
body:
White slid further in 2025: across 17 games his output fell to 103 points and RB35, down from 148.6 (RB25) in 2024 and a workhorse 203.9 (RB4) in 2023. His carry share has cratered as snap rate dropped from 78.4 to 51.1 percent, and the pass-game value that once defined him eroded too, with six receiving scores and 393 yards in 2024 becoming zero TDs and 218 yards on a 0.63 yards-per-route mark. He ran efficiently at 4.33 per carry with a positive 0.054 EPA, but only 1.51 yards after contact flags a back living on his blocking.

Now in Washington, the former third-round Buccaneer (Pick 91, 2022) joins a new backfield where his early-down role is hardly guaranteed and a receiving rebound is the swing factor. His 8.3 percent target share and eight goal-line carries hint at usable PPR volume if he wins the passing-down job, but the trajectory is clearly downward. Treat him as a low-end flex and bench-depth dart, not a weekly starter.
